Fee Change Alert MIAX Emerald January 30, 2026 | 01:00 PM MIAX Emerald Options Exchange - February 1, 2026 Fee Update Effective February 1, 2026, pending filing with the SEC, MIAX Emerald Options will amend its fee schedule as follows: For Members in Priority Customer Tier 4 that have achieved above 0.90% of Total Market Maker sides volume and above 0.60% of Priority Customer, Maker sides volume, both thresholds as a percentage of OCC Customer volume (CTCV), Priority Customer Simple Orders that are providing liquidity and trading against an affiliated Market Maker will be ($0.43) for Penny classes and ($0.95) for non-Penny classes Attached is a highlighted summary of the February 2026 fee changes for MIAX Emerald Options. title: MIAX Exchange Group - Options Markets - Fee Changes to Establish Fees for the 1-Minute Interval Intra-Day Open-Close Report and the Update to the MIAX Options Exchange 10-Minute Interval Intra-Day Open Close Report Fee
raw_text:
MIAX Options — Fee Change Alert — MIAX Exchange Group - Options Markets - Fee Changes to Establish Fees for the 1-Minute Interval Intra-Day Open-Close Report and the Update to the MIAX Options Exchange 10-Minute Interval Intra-Day Open Close Report Fee
Fee Change Alert MIAX Options January 30, 2026 | 09:26 AM MIAX Exchange Group - Options Markets - Fee Changes to Establish Fees for the 1-Minute Interval Intra-Day Open-Close Report and the Update to the MIAX Options Exchange 10-Minute Interval Intra-Day Open Close Report Fee As previously announced in the January 23, 2026 Alert , MIAX Options Exchange, MIAX Pearl Options Exchange, MIAX Emerald Options Exchange, and MIAX Sapphire Options Exchange (collectively, “the Exchanges”) will make the new 1-Minute Interval Intra-Day Open-Close Report (the “1-Minute Open-Close Report”) available to subscribers beginning February 2, 2026. Effective February 2, 2026, pending filings with the U.S. Securities and Exchange Commission, the Exchanges will amend the following fees: MIAX Options Exchange Monthly subscription to the 1-Minute Open-Close Report: $9,000 per month Ad hoc request for historical 1-Minute Open-Close Report data: $4,000 per request per month A request can be for any number of months beginning with January 2013 for which the data is available Monthly subscription to the 10-Minute Interval Intra-Day Open-Close Report (the “10-Minute Open-Close Report”): $3,000 per month MIAX Pearl Options Exchange, MIAX Emerald Options Exchange and MIAX Sapphire Options Exchange Monthly subscription to the 1-Minute Open-Close Report: $6,000 per month Ad hoc request for historical 1-Minute Open-Close Report data: $2,500 per request per month Requests can be for any number of months beginning with March 2017 (for MIAX Pearl Options Exchange), March 2019 (for MIAX Emerald Options Exchange), or August 2024 (for MIAX Sapphire Options Exchange) for which the data is available Monthly subscribers to the 1-Minute Open-Close Report may request an End-of-Day subscription and/or 10-Minute Open-Close Report subscription for no additional charge for each of the Exchanges Qualifying academic purchases of historical 1-Minute Open-Close Report data for each of the Exchanges: $4,500 per year for the first year; $375 per month for each additional month For additional information, please contact MIAX Sales at Sales@miaxglobal.com or (609) 897-8177.

title: MIAX Sapphire Options Exchange - Updated MEO Interface Specification to Support Upcoming Selective Liquidity Auto Purge (SLAP) Enhancement
raw_text:
MIAX Sapphire — Technical Alert, Trading Alert, Regulatory Alert — MIAX Sapphire Options Exchange - Updated MEO Interface Specification to Support Upcoming Selective Liquidity Auto Purge (SLAP) Enhancement
Technical Alert, Trading Alert, Regulatory Alert MIAX Sapphire January 28, 2026 | 03:49 PM MIAX Sapphire Options Exchange - Updated MEO Interface Specification to Support Upcoming Selective Liquidity Auto Purge (SLAP) Enhancement The MIAX Sapphire Options Exchange MIAX Express Order (MEO) Interface Specification has been updated to support the Selective Liquidity Auto Purge (SLAP) Functionality enhancement. SLAP Overview : This optional feature enhances the Mass Cancellation functionality by providing Members with the ability to remove a subset of its orders in the System This feature requires certification prior to being used in production With the introduction of a new field “SLAP Codes” in the Bulk Liquidity Message, Members can specify custom SLAP codes, numbered 1 through 8, on some or all of its liquidity, providing more granular control of the Liquidity Mass Cancel Request when attempting to “Selectively Purge” specific liquidity The MEO Interface Specification v1.2 has been updated as follows : New field “SLAP Codes” in Simple Bulk Liquidity Message (Auto Replace A-R Order, Standard Binary Order – New, Standard Binary Order – Cancel/Replace) New field “SLAP Codes” and new Mass Cancel Scope in Liquidity Mass Cancel Request Enhanced Liquidity Protection Reset Request message that includes new field “SLAP Codes” and new field “Scope” to specify Liquidity Protection Reset Scope type New SLAP Protection Trigger Notification New cancel reason due to SLAP in Cancel Notification New status codes subject to SLAP in the following response messages: Simple Bulk Liquidity Message Response Liquidity Mass Cancel Response Liquidity Protection Reset Response Updated specifications can be found at MIAX Sapphire Options Interface Specifications Important Dates : An activation schedule for these changes will be announced in a future alert These changes are currently available in the MIAX Sapphire Options Exchange's Firm Test Bed (FTB2) environment Please contact MIAX Trading Operations at TradingOperations@miaxglobal.com or (609) 897-7302 to update configurations, arrange interface testing and schedule certification.

title: MIAX Exchange Group - Options Markets - Introducing Availability of 1-Minute Interval Intra-Day Open-Close Reports
raw_text:
MIAX Emerald — Regulatory Alert, Technical Alert, Trading Alert — MIAX Exchange Group - Options Markets - Introducing Availability of 1-Minute Interval Intra-Day Open-Close Reports
Regulatory Alert, Technical Alert, Trading Alert MIAX Emerald January 23, 2026 | 02:16 PM MIAX Exchange Group - Options Markets - Introducing Availability of 1-Minute Interval Intra-Day Open-Close Reports The MIAX Options, MIAX Pearl Options, MIAX Emerald Options and MIAX Sapphire Options Exchanges are pleased to announce the introduction of new 1-Minute Interval Intra-Day Open-Close Reports, which will be available for subscription beginning Monday, February 2, 2026. The 1-Minute Interval Intra-Day Open-Close Reports, available separately for each exchange, are designed to provide aggregate trade quantity and volume data by origin, buy/sell, and open/close criteria for each option series traded on the MIAX Options, MIAX Pearl Options, MIAX Emerald Options or MIAX Sapphire Options Exchanges. The 1-Minute Interval Intra-Day Open-Close Reports will be available via the MIAX SFTP site. The Intraday file is a cumulative file produced in 1-minute intervals throughout the trading day. The 1-Minute Interval Intra-Day Open-Close Reports are being offered in addition to the existing 10-Minute Interval Intra-Day Open-Close Reports and End-of-Day Open-Close Reports. 1-Minute Interval Intra-Day Open-Close history is available for each Exchange dating back to the following years and months. MIAX Options: January 2013, MIAX Pearl Options: March 2017, MIAX Emerald Options: March 2019 and MIAX Sapphire Options: August 2024. Further details regarding Reports can be found at MIAX Reports , Pearl Reports , Emerald Reports & Sapphire Reports . Monthly subscriptions are required to access the reports. Each Exchange plans to file proposed subscription fees with the SEC prior to the availability of the reports. The fee schedules are available at MIAX Options Fees , MIAX Pearl Options Fees , MIAX Emerald Options Fees and MIAX Sapphire Options Fees . Firms can request reports and specifications by contacting MIAX Market Data at MarketDataSupport@miaxglobal.com . Please send technical questions to TradingOperations@miaxglobal.com or (609) 897-7302. For additional information, please contact MIAX Sales at Sales@miaxglobal.com or (609) 897-8177.

raw_text:
MIAX Sapphire — Technical Alert, Trading Alert, Regulatory Alert — MIAX Sapphire Options Exchange - Trading Floor - Virtual Trading Floor (VTF) Test - February 28, 2026
Technical Alert, Trading Alert, Regulatory Alert MIAX Sapphire January 21, 2026 | 03:43 PM MIAX Sapphire Options Exchange - Trading Floor - Virtual Trading Floor (VTF) Test - February 28, 2026 As previously communicated, MIAX Sapphire will provide access to a Virtual Trading Floor (VTF) system to be used in the event the physical trading floor is inaccessible. The first 2026 test will occur on Saturday, February 28, 2026, from 8:30 a.m. to 11:30 a.m. The test will use the VTF system in combination with the MIAX Sapphire Production Environment to allow Market Makers and Floor Brokers to familiarize themselves with the VTF system. Note: the VTF system operates independently and does not require integration with market maker or floor broker systems. The use of the VTF system along with the Sapphire production systems for this exercise is solely for replicating potential trading scenarios. Please send an email to TradingOperations@miaxglobal.com once you have successfully completed testing. Floor Broker Test Plan : Log in and confirm audio Log in and confirm video access Enter a trade into the Sapphire Trading system from your remote location Log into Member Firm Portal from your remote location and perform a post trade allocation Market Maker Test Plan : Log in and confirm audio Log in and confirm video access Respond to an order and participate in a trade Confirm receipt of trade allocation via your firms network Confirm receipt of trade allocation via the trade drop if applicable Log into Member Firm Portal from your remote location Additional test dates will be provided through future Alerts. For additional information, please contact MIAX Trading Operations at TradingOperations@miaxglobal.com or (609) 897-7302. Regulatory inquiries should be directed to Regulatory@miaxglobal.com or (609) 897-7309. Sales inquiries should be directed to Sales@miaxglobal.com , (609) 897-8177 or visit miaxglobal.com .

title: MIAX Exchange Group - Options Markets - Listing of Monday and Wednesday Weekly Expirations for Qualified Securities
raw_text:
MIAX Pearl — Regulatory Alert, Listing Alert, Trading Alert — MIAX Exchange Group - Options Markets - Listing of Monday and Wednesday Weekly Expirations for Qualified Securities
Regulatory Alert, Listing Alert, Trading Alert MIAX Pearl January 20, 2026 | 03:10 PM MIAX Exchange Group - Options Markets - Listing of Monday and Wednesday Weekly Expirations for Qualified Securities The MIAX Options Exchanges have filed with the SEC to amend the Short Term Option Series Program of each exchange to permit the listing of up to two Monday and Wednesday expirations for options on certain individual stocks or Exchange-Traded Fund (ETF) Shares (“Qualifying Securities”). On January 26, 2026, the MIAX Options Exchanges plan to begin listing Qualifying Securities with the following 2026 expiration dates: February 2nd, 4th, 9th, and 11th. Please note that GOOGL will be excluded from the February 4th date because of an earnings announcement. A Qualifying Security is an individual stock or ETF that, on a quarterly basis: (1) has an underlying security, as measured on the last day of the prior calendar quarter, with: (A) a market capitalization of greater than 700 billion dollars for an individual stock based on the closing price, or (B) Assets under Management (“AUM”) greater than 50 billion dollars for an Exchange-Traded Fund Share based on net asset value (“NAV”); (2) has monthly options volume, as measured by sides traded in the last month preceding the quarter end, of greater than 10 million options; (3) has a position limit of at least 250,000 contracts; and (4) participates in the Penny Interval Program. A Qualifying Security expiration would not be listed on a day when there will be an earnings announcement that takes place after market close. The following securities are anticipated to be Qualifying Securities for 1st Quarter 2026: Alphabet, Inc. (GOOGL) Apple Inc. (AAPL) Amazon.com, Inc. (AMZN) Broadcom Inc. (AVGO) iShares Bitcoin Trust ETF (IBIT) Meta Platforms, Inc. (META) Microsoft Corporation (MSFT) NVIDIA Corporation (NVDA) Tesla, Inc. (TSLA) Each calendar quarter, the above criteria will be reassessed to determine eligibility for the following quarter as a Qualifying Security. The MIAX Option Exchanges will make the list of Qualifying Securities that meet the above criteria available on our website by the close of business on the first trading day of each quarter. Please contact MIAX Options Listings with any questions at Listings@MIAXOptions.com or (609) 897-7308.

title: Member Guidance for Message and Packet Activity Towards MIAX Exchanges
raw_text:
MIAX Emerald — Trading Alert, Regulatory Alert — Member Guidance for Message and Packet Activity Towards MIAX Exchanges
Trading Alert, Regulatory Alert MIAX Emerald January 16, 2026 | 12:10 PM Member Guidance for Message and Packet Activity Towards MIAX Exchanges Regarding message and packet activity for MIAX Options, MIAX Pearl Options, MIAX Emerald Options, and MIAX Sapphire Options, Members are prohibited from behaviors that have a negative impact on the normal operation or designed performance profile of MIAX trading systems. Members are specifically prohibited from using SesM-TCP test messages, SesM-TCP Heartbeat messages of more than 50 per second per port, corrupting packets that result in FCS errors, and/or intentionally splitting application layer messages into two or more packets when interacting with the MIAX trading systems. Please refer to the follow Regulatory Circulars for guidance on accessing Exchange systems: MIAX Options RC 2026-05 MIAX Pearl Options RC 2026-05 MIAX Emerald Options RC 2026-05 MIAX Sapphire Options RC 2026-05 For questions regarding transmission sent to MIAX, contact Trading Operations at TradingOperations@miaxglobal.com or (609) 897-7302. raw_text:
MIAX Options — Trading Alert, Technical Alert — MIAX Exchange Group - Options and Equities Exchanges - VPN Maintenance Scheduled for January 10, 2026
Trading Alert, Technical Alert MIAX Options January 06, 2026 | 10:01 AM MIAX Exchange Group - Options and Equities Exchanges - VPN Maintenance Scheduled for January 10, 2026 The MIAX Options and Equities Exchanges will perform VPN maintenance on Saturday, January 10, 2026. The maintenance will impact firms that utilize VPNs to access the Firm Test Beds (FTB1 & FTB2) environments and MIAX's Secondary SFTP Server. Firms with direct connectivity will not be impacted. For further information, please contact MIAX Trading Operations at TradingOperations@MIAXGlobal.com or (609) 897-7302.

title: MIAX Pearl Equities Exchange - February 1, 2026 Fee Changes
raw_text:
MIAX Pearl Equities — Fee Change Alert — MIAX Pearl Equities Exchange - February 1, 2026 Fee Changes
Fee Change Alert MIAX Pearl Equities January 30, 2026 | 12:59 PM MIAX Pearl Equities Exchange - February 1, 2026 Fee Changes Effective February 1, 2026, pending filing with the SEC, MIAX Pearl Equities will make the below described changes to the Market Quoting Program. MIAX Pearl Equities will also employ a new billing methodology to comply with new Regulation NMS Rule 610(d). As previously mentioned in the December 30, 2025 Alert , beginning February 1, 2026 and in compliance with new Regulation NMS Rule 610(d), MIAX Pearl Equities will begin calculating pricing tiers for the current month based on the prior month’s trading activity. For example, February tier qualifications will be based on trading activity in January. New Equity Members of MIAX Pearl Equities will default to base pricing tiers as no prior month activity will be available. The minimum threshold to qualify for the Market Quoting Program will be increased from an average of at least 750 securities per day to an average of at least 900 securities per day Note: The updated minimum threshold of 900 securities will need to be achieved in February 2026 to qualify for and receive the rebate under this program for transactions in March 2026 The required “Percent Time at NBBO” will remain greater than or equal to 50% The enhanced rebate received for this program will remain ($0.0026) Rebates for this program are applicable to the following Fee Codes: Liquidity Code AA, “Adds Liquidity, Displayed Order (Tape A)” Liquidity Code AB, “Adds Liquidity, Displayed Order (Tape B)” Liquidity Code AC, “Adds Liquidity, Displayed Order (Tape C)” Liquidity Code EA, “Early Trading Session, Adds Liquidity, Displayed Order (Tape A)” Liquidity Code EB, “Early Trading Session, Adds Liquidity, Displayed Order (Tape B)” Liquidity Code EC, “Early Trading Session, Adds Liquidity, Displayed Order (Tape C)” Liquidity Code FA, “Late Trading Session, Adds Liquidity, Displayed Order (Tape A)” Liquidity Code FB, “Late Trading Session, Adds Liquidity, Displayed Order (Tape B)” Liquidity Code FC, “Late Trading Session, Adds Liquidity, Displayed Order (Tape C)” For full details, please review the updated Market Quality Securities list (effective February 1, 2026) and MIAX Pearl Equities Fee Schedule posted on the MIAX website. To learn more about MIAX Pearl Equities, please contact Sales@miaxglobal.com , (609) 897-8177 or visit miaxglobal.com. For further information, please contact MIAX Trading Operations at TradingOperations@miaxglobal.com or (609) 897-7302. MIAX Pearl Equities Highlight of Fee Changes 02012026

title: MIAX Futures Exchange - Upcoming Changes to the Minneapolis Hard Red Spring Wheat Contract
raw_text:
MIAX Futures — Technical Alert, Clearing Alert, Trading Alert — MIAX Futures Exchange - Upcoming Changes to the Minneapolis Hard Red Spring Wheat Contract
Technical Alert, Clearing Alert, Trading Alert MIAX Futures January 30, 2026 | 11:03 AM MIAX Futures Exchange - Upcoming Changes to the Minneapolis Hard Red Spring Wheat Contract As previously announced in the March 4, 2025 Alert , MIAX Futures will implement changes that will impact the terms and conditions of the Minneapolis Hard Red Spring Wheat (Minneapolis HRSW) contract effective with the September 2026 delivery period (July 24, 2026). These amendments have received formal CFTC approval and include the following material changes: MIAX Futures will convert the delivery instrument for Minneapolis HRSW Contracts from Warehouse Receipts to Shipping Certificates. This conversion will include automatic monthly storage pay/collects. Please see additional details in the previously announced September 29, 2025 Alert . Hard red spring wheat delivered against a Minneapolis HRSW Contract will be required to have a minimum falling number of 200. Hard red spring wheat delivered against a Minneapolis HRSW Contract will be required to list a country of origin designation. MIAX Futures will be adding a North Dakota / Minnesota Territory interior delivery point for hard red spring wheat delivered against a Minneapolis HRSW Contract. MIAX Futures will be increasing the maximum load-out charges for hard red spring wheat delivered against a Minneapolis HRSW Contract from 8 cents to 10 cents per bushel, and the maximum storage charges from 7 cents to 8 cents per bushel per month. For the complete set of changes, please see the CFTC approved rules on the website pending implementation here . For additional information on Shipping Certificates and Regular Elevator information, see the FAQ here . For any additional information, please contact MIAX Futures Market Regulation Department at MIAXFuturesMarketReg@miaxglobal.com . To revise a current MIAX Alert subscription or to register to receive alerts for MIAX Futures, please visit MIAX Email Subscriptions .

title: NYSE - Direct Debit via DTCC and Invoice Changes Effective April 2026
raw_text:
NYSE - Direct Debit via DTCC and Invoice Changes Effective April 2026
Markets: NYSE; Services: Trading, Post Trade, Market Data
Beginning in April 2026, NYSE will move to Direct Debit via DTCC ("Direct Debit") as the default payment method for certain fees and introduce a new invoice format that summarizes fees on executions. DTCC Direct Debit for Transaction, Membership, and Session Fees The previously announced move to Direct Debit via DTCC ("Direct Debit") for Transaction, Membership, and Session Fees will now be implemented in April 2026. Firms may opt out of Direct Debit by completing the Direct Debit Opt-Out Request form and returning it to CRS at crs@nyse.com by March 24, 2026. Invoice Format Update Beginning with the April 2026 bill, a new invoice format will be introduced. This updated format will summarize fees by the liquidity tag returned on executions. A full list of liquidity tags may be found on the NYSE Pillar Reason Codes and Liquidity Indicators . Please contact rmteam@nyse.com with any questions about these changes.

title: NYSE Arca Options - Cash-Settled FLEX ETF Options Available February 2026
raw_text:
NYSE Arca Options - Cash-Settled FLEX ETF Options Available February 2026
Markets: NYSE ARCA OPTIONS; Services: Trading, Post Trade, Market Data
Beginning February 2, 2026, NYSE Arca Options will offer 'cash' settlement as a flexible term for select eligible issues , pursuant to NYSE Arca Options Rule 5.32-O(f). To qualify, the underlying ETF must have an average daily notional value of $500 million or more and a national average daily volume of at least 4,680,000 shares (measured over the prior 6-month period). title: NYSE American Options - NYSE Pillar Hardware Upgrade - Reminder
raw_text:
NYSE American Options - NYSE Pillar Hardware Upgrade - Reminder
Markets: NYSE AMERICAN OPTIONS; Services: Trading, Post Trade, Market Data
As previously announced , on Monday, March 2, 2026 , NYSE American Options will upgrade hardware for its NYSE Pillar technology platform. The migration will be an immediate cutover, with no parallel production period. Proprietary Market Data Feeds NYSE American Options Top (BBO and Trades), Deep and Complex (excluding Imbalance and Summary) feeds will increase from 17 to 24 Publisher, Refresh and Retransmission channels. Please refer to NYSE Proprietary Data IP Addresses for new channel information. The Certification environment is currently available on the new hardware. Customers should use the start of day symbol reference data for symbol mapping information. There will be no XDP format changes. Key Dates Industry Test #2 Saturday, February 7, 2026 Industry Test #3 Saturday, February 28, 2026 New Hardware in Production Monday, March 2, 2026 Heartbeats on new market data channels Monday, March 2, 2026 Test symbols on new market data channels Monday, March 9, 2026 DR Industry Test Saturday, March 28, 2026 New hardware in DR Monday, March 30, 2026 Production symbols on new channels Tuesday, March 31, 2026 Further information may be found in the FAQs . Please contact rmteam@nyse.com with any questions about this update. Contact Technology Member Services tms@nyse.com Connectivity support@nyse.com © 2026 NYSE. All Rights Reserved

NYSE Options: Optional NYSE Pillar Order Entry Restrictions
Markets: NYSE AMERICAN OPTIONS, NYSE ARCA OPTIONS; Services: Trading, Post Trade, Market Data
Beginning February 2, 2026 on NYSE American Options and February 4, 2026 on NYSE Arca Options, the Exchanges will introduce optional order restrictions that firms may enable on the NYSE Pillar Gateway order entry sessions. Firms may activate any or all of the restrictions. This functionality will be available for testing on the NYSE Pillar Certification environment on January 26, 2026 . NYSE Pillar Options Order Entry Restrictions Using the NYSE Pillar NGW Session Request Form, firms may configure their FIX and Binary order entry sessions with the following optional restrictions: CustomerOrFirm Require a specific value in the CustomerOrFirm field at order entry. If CustomerOrFirm field contains any other value, Pillar will reject it with: R038: Invalid CustomerOrFirm. CMTA/ClearingFirm Require a specific value in the CMTA/ClearingFirm field at order entry. If CMTA/ClearingFirm field contains a different value or is missing, Pillar will reject it with: R047: Invalid ClearingFirm. Account/UserData Require an Account/UserData field be populated at order entry. If Account/UserData field is left blank, Pillar will reject the message with: R167: Invalid UserData. Markets: NYSE AMERICAN OPTIONS; Services: Trading, Post Trade, Market Data
Pursuant to NYSE American Options Rule 903G(c)(3)(ii), the Exchange offers 'cash' settlement as a flexible term for select eligible issues . To qualify, the underlying security must have an average daily notional value of $500 million or more and a national average daily volume of at least 4,680,000 shares (measured over the prior 6-month period). If an underlying ETF ceases to satisfy the criteria, any new position overlying the ETF will only settle by physical delivery, and any open cash-settled FLEX ETF Option positions may be traded only to close the position. title: NYSE - DTCC Direct Debit for Transaction, Membership, and Session Fees
raw_text:
NYSE - DTCC Direct Debit for Transaction, Membership, and Session Fees
Markets: NYSE; Services: Trading
Beginning in March 2026, NYSE ("the Exchange") will move to Direct Debit via DTCC ("Direct Debit") as the default payment method for Transaction, Membership, and Session Fees. This convenient process will offer firms the ability to pay fees via automatic debit from the firm's clearing account at DTCC. Firms may opt out of Direct Debit by completing this form and returning it to CRS at crs@nyse.com by February 20, 2026 . Firms with multiple MPIDs must contact CRS to establish a default billing MPID and associated DTCC number, from which firm level fees will be collected. Firms that use the Universal Flip Process at DTCC should also contact CRS. DTCC Direct Debit for Transaction, Membership, and Session Fees The process for Direct Debit, beginning in March 2026 will be as follows: The Exchange will send monthly electronic invoices, generally by the fifth business day of each month, for fees generated in the prior month. The Exchange will subsequently initiate the debit of any fees due to the Exchange for the prior month on or about the 15th day of the month. Firms can dispute an invoice in writing by the 15th of the month or the following business day if the 15th is not a business day. If the disputed amount is $10,000 or greater, the Exchange will not include the disputed amount in the debit pending resolution of the dispute. For further information please see https://www.nyse.com/trade/trading-information#direct-debit . Please contact rmteam@nyse.com with any questions about this change.

title: NYSE Group: Reminder - New Tier Calculation Process (SEC Rule 610 Changes), Effective February 2, 2026
raw_text:
NYSE Group: Reminder - New Tier Calculation Process (SEC Rule 610 Changes), Effective February 2, 2026
Markets: NYSE, NYSE AMERICAN EQUITIES, NYSE ARCA EQUITIES, NYSE NATIONAL, NYSE TEXAS; Services: Trading, Post Trade, Market Data
Beginning February 2, 2026, NYSE Group equities exchanges will calculate monthly pricing tiers based on the prior month's trading activity as required by recent SEC Rule 610 amendments , as previously announced . New members will receive base rates during their first month of trading. Existing Daily Tier Reports will continue to display T+1 volume activity; however, this data will now be used to determine the following month's rates. For additional information, please refer to the SEC Final Rule . Please contact your NYSE Relationship Manager or email rmteam@nyse.com with any questions about this update.

title: NYSE - New Security Notification for Symbols ALUB and ALUB WS
raw_text:
NYSE - New Security Notification for Symbols ALUB and ALUB WS
Markets: NYSE; Services: Trading
NYSE has determined that the Class A Ordinary Shares issued by Alussa Energy Acquisition Corp. II is part of a new security offering that will trade under the ticker ALUB with CUSIP G0233J100 on January 6, 2026. As this new offering has not previously traded on any listing market and has no prior day's closing price, Regulation SHO Rule 201 will not apply to security ALUB until its second day of trading on NYSE. Further, NYSE Rule 123D(d) will apply and the security will be in a regulatory halt until the NYSE has opened trading. NYSE has determined that the Redeemable Warrants, each whole warrant exercisable for one Class A ordinary share at an exercise price of $11.50 per share issued by Alussa Energy Acquisition Corp. II is part of a new security offering that will trade under the ticker ALUB WS with CUSIP G0233J118 on January 6, 2026. As this new offering has not previously traded on any listing market and has no prior day's closing price, Regulation SHO Rule 201 will not apply to security ALUB WS until its second day of trading on NYSE. Further, NYSE Rule 123D(d) will apply and the security will be in a regulatory halt until the NYSE has opened trading.

title: New OPRA Dynamic Rebalance Symbol Distribution – Industry Tests and Go-Live Date: November 23, 2026
raw_text:
New OPRA Dynamic Rebalance Symbol Distribution – Industry Tests and Go-Live Date: November 23, 2026
January 7, 2026 To: OPRA Multicast Subscribers Subject: New OPRA Dynamic Rebalance Symbol Distribution – Industry Tests and Go-Live Date: November 23, 2026 What’s New: As previously announced, OPRA is implementing a change to the message traffic distribution and balancing method it currently uses. After receiving survey feedback from over 40 OPRA Subscribers, to provide ample time for all OPRA consumers to be ready, the go-live implementation date will be November 23, 2026. The following link to the Dynamic Rebalance Plan includes information pertaining to: • Daily series mapping file • New real time Reference Routing message • Implementation approach and examples Further detailed information will be provided in future OPRA notifications. Certification (Cert) System Testing: • Begins Monday, August 24, 2026 (Functional testing) Planned Industry Test dates: • Saturday, August 29, 2026 (Functional testing) • Saturday October 10, 2026 (Functional, Failure/Recovery, and Capacity testing) • Saturday, October 31, 2026 (Intraday fallback to November 20, 2026, symbol routing) • Saturday, November 7, 2026 (Final Functional testing) • Saturday, November 21, 2026 (Confidence Test prior to Go-Live) • Monday, November 23, 2026: Activation Go-Live Documentation: OPRA specifications and FAQ will be available by the end of this month. Technical Inquiries • NMS Product Management Support Email: CTA-OPRA-Support@siac.com • NMS Product Management Support Line: 212-656-8177, Option 2 • NMS Product Management Phone Support Hours: 8:00 AM - 5:00 PM ET Securities Industry Automation Corporation 11 Wall Street, New York, NY 10005 Securities Industry Automation Corporation 11 Wall Street, New York, NY 10005
